2605.07724.title: Curated Synthetic Data Doesn't Have to Collapse: A Theoretical Study of Generative Retraining with Pluralistic Preferences
2605.07724.paper_summary: Recursive retraining of generative models poses a critical representation challenge: when synthetic outputs are curated based on a fixed reward signal, the model tends to collapse onto a narrow set of outputs that over-optimize that objective. Prior work suggests that such collapse is unavoidable without adding real data into the mix. We revisit this conclusion from an alignment perspective and show that collapse can be mitigated through curation based on multiple reward functions. We formalize the dynamics of recursive training under heterogeneous preferences and prove that, under certain conditions, the model converges to a stable distribution that allocates probability mass across competing high-reward regions. The limiting distribution preserves diversity and provably satisfies a weighted Nash bargaining solution, offering a formal interpretation of value aggregation in synthetic retraining loops.
2605.07724.paper_summary_zh: 遞歸再訓練生成模型帶來了一個關鍵的表徵挑戰：當合成輸出是基於固定的獎勵信號進行策劃時，模型往往會崩潰到一組狹窄的輸出，過度優化該目標。先前的研究表明，若不將真實數據納入其中，這種崩潰是不可避免的。我們從對齊的角度重新審視這一結論，並顯示通過基於多個獎勵函數的策劃可以減輕崩潰。我們形式化了在異質偏好下的遞歸訓練動態，並證明在某些條件下，模型收斂到一個穩定的分佈，將概率質量分配到競爭的高獎勵區域。極限分佈保留了多樣性，並可證明滿足加權的納什議價解，為合成再訓練循環中的價值聚合提供了正式解釋。
2605.07724.author: Ali Falahati et.al.
2605.07724.authors: Ali Falahati, Mohammad Mohammadi Amiri, Kate Larson, Lukasz Golab
